IRS990EZ.PrimaryExemptPurposeTxt: THE MISSION OF THE ASSOCIATION IS TO PROVIDE LEADERSHIP FOR EXCELLENCE IN ALL ASPECTS OF JOURNALISM AND MASS COMMUNICATION EDUCATION BY THE ACHIEVEMENT AND MAINTENANCE OF THE HIGHEST STANDARDS IN JOURNALISM AND MASS COMMUNICATION EDUCATION IN THE NATION'S COLLEGES AND UNIVERSITIES.

ProgramSrvcAccomplishmentGrp.DescriptionProgramSrvcAccomTxt: TO PROMOTE EXCELLENCE IN ADMINISTRATION OF JOURNALISM AND MASS COMMUNICATION EDUCATION THROUGH WORKSHOPS, MEETINGS, PUBLICATIONS, AND GRANTS TO MEMBER SCHOOLS.

KNOXVILLE 62-6001636 2 0 0

ANY UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE OF THE UNITED STATES MAY BE ELECTED TO INSTITUTIONAL MEMBERSHIP AND BE REPRESENTED BY AN EXECUTIVE WHO DEVOTES A MAJOR PORTION OF HIS/HER EMPLOYMENT TO ADMINISTERING A SEQUENCE, DEPARTMENT, DIVISION, SCHOOL OR COLLEGE OF JOURNALISM OR MASS COMMUNICATIONS IN SUCH UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE, AND IS DESIGNATED BY THE CHIEF EXECUTIVE OF SAID INSTITUTION AS ITS REPRESENTATIVE, PROVIDED THAT SUCH INSTITUTION IS FULLY ACCREDITED BY A RECOGNIZED REGIONAL ACCREDITING ASSOCIATION AND PROVIDED FURTHER THAT THE INSTITUTION'S COURSE OFFERINGS IN THE VARIOUS ASPECTS OF JOURNALISM/MASS COMMUNICATION CONSTITUTE A MAJOR FIELD OF STUDY IN WHICH AN UNDERGRADUATE OR GRADUATE STUDENT MAY TAKE A BACCALAUREATE OR MASTER'S DEGREE. INSTITUTIONAL MEMBERS HAVE VOTING RIGHTS. ANY UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE OUTSIDE THE UNITED STATES MAY BE ELECTED TO INTERNATIONAL MEMBERSHIP AND BE REPRESENTED BY A JOURNALISM OR MASS COMMUNICATION EDUCATION EXECUTIVE WHO DEVOTES A MAJOR PORTION OF HIS/HER EMPLOYMENT TO ADMINISTERING A SEQUENCE, DEPARTMENT, DIVISION, SCHOOL OR COLLEGE OF JOURNALISM OR MASS COMMUNICATIONS IN SUCH UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE, AND IS DESIGNATED BY THE CHIEF EXECUTIVE OF SAID INSTITUTION AS ITS REPRESENTATIVE. INTERNATION MEMBERS DO NOT HAVE VOTING RIGHTS.

THE ORGANIZATION'S INTERNATIONAL MEMBERS CONSIST OF UNIVERSITIES AND COLLEGES ORGANIZED OUTSIDE OF THE UNITED STATES, WHICH WOULD STILL QUALIFY AS EXEMPT UNDER IRC SECTION 509(A)(1) AND 170(B)(1)(A). IN ADDITION, INTERNATIONAL MEMBERS DO NOT HAVE VOTING RIGHTS.
INTERNATIONAL MEMBERS DO NOT HAVE VOTING RIGHTS.

THE ORGANIZATION'S INTERNATIONAL MEMBERS CONSIST OF UNIVERSITIES AND COLLEGES ORGANIZED OUTSIDE OF THE UNITED STATES, WHICH WOULD STILL QUALIFY AS EXEMPT UNDER IRC SECTION 509(A)(1) AND 170(B)(1)(A). IN ADDITION, INTERNATIONAL MEMBERS DO NOT HAVE VOTING RIGHTS.

EACH INSTITUTIONAL MEMBER HAS ONE REPRESENTATIVE. THESE REPRESENTATIVES ELECT THE ORGANIZATION'S OFFICERS EACH YEAR THROUGH ONLINE BALLOT. OFFICERS CAN SERVE FROM ONE TO THREE YEAR TERMS, DEPENDING ON THE SPECIFIC DUTIES OF THE OFFICE.

THE ASSOCIATION DOES NOT PROVIDE MONIES BACK TO ITS MEMBERS, BUT PROVIDES RESOURCES, WORKSHOPS, SURVEY RESULTS AND INFORMATION TO ENHANCE JOURNALISM AND MASS COMMUNICATIONS ADMINISTRATIVE WORK ON THEIR HOME CAMPUSES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: EXPENSES WEBSITE DESIGN 315 AFFILIATE DUES 12,000 SALARY SURVEY 30,000 CONFERENCES AND MEETINGS 30,612 TOTAL 72,927

THE MISSION OF THE ASSOCIATION IS TO PROVIDE LEADERSHIP FOR EXCELLENCE IN ALL ASPECTS OF JOURNALISM AND MASS COMMUNICATION EDUCATION BY THE ACHIEVEMENT AND MAINTENANCE OF THE HIGHEST STANDARDS IN JOURNALISM AND MASS COMMUNICATION EDUCATION IN THE NATION'S COLLEGES AND UNIVERSITIES.
